title: asphodelaceae
intro: Asphodelaceae is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the asphodel or asphodel family, that consists of approximately 260-300 species. This family is characterized by its diverse range of plants, including asphodels, agaves, and yuccas, often found in Mediterranean and subtropical regions. Many species within this family are known for their striking, architectural foliage and showy inflorescences, which can range from white to yellow to red in color. Asphodelaceae is an important and widespread family, found in many parts of the world, including Africa, Asia, Europe, and the Americas.
